petitioner_background: The petitioner is a musician/composer from the Philippines seeking classification as an alien of extraordinary ability in the arts under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Act. He has a past record of success and national acclaim in the Philippines' music industry. He intends to compose, arrange, and produce material in the United States.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was sustained and the petition approved. The director had denied the petition on the grounds that the petitioner had not established he would continue employment in his field of expertise in the United States or that his entry would substantially benefit the United States prospectively. On appeal, the petitioner submitted a statement detailing plans on how he intends to continue his work in the United States, combined with a letter from a prospective employer stating they are interested in having the petitioner compose, arrange, and produce material for their clientele. The AAO found this adequate to demonstrate intent to continue work in his area of expertise, and concluded it was reasonable based on his past record of success and national acclaim in the Philippines' music industry that his entry would substantially benefit the arts in the United States. The appellate submission overcame the stated grounds for denial.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act, 8 U.S.C. § 1153(b)(1)(A)
